jobs.purpose: Rotate one SA's secret programmatically. Invoked on-demand (via `service-account:rotate` command OR the platform-admin rotate-secret HTTP endpoint) or by scheduled expiry checks. Generates a fresh 32-byte secret, bcrypt-hashes it, updates `secret_hash`/`secret_rotated_at`/`expires_at` inside a transaction. Fires ServiceAccountSecretRotated. The plaintext secret is returned to the caller inline (job payload is the caller-consumable response); it is NOT persisted anywhere.
steps: Load the ServiceAccount row via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE.
Refuse when is_enabled=false + trigger != 'incident' (rotating a disabled SA is usually a mistake; incident-response wants to rotate a disabled SA before re-enabling).
Generate 32 cryptographically-random bytes via SecretRotator::generate(32).
bcrypt-hash the plaintext at cost 12 via SecretRotator::hash($plain).
UPDATE service_accounts SET secret_hash = $newHash, secret_rotated_at = now(), expires_at = now() + INTERVAL '$ttlDays days' WHERE id = ?.
Emit `academorix.service_account.rotated` metric (label: trigger).
Fire ServiceAccountSecretRotated event via after-commit dispatch.
Return the plaintext to the caller (encrypted in transit via the caller's own transport).

jobs.purpose: Weekly (Mondays 08:00 UTC) enumerate every SA whose `expires_at` falls inside the next `service-accounts.rotation.warning_days_before_expiry` window (default 14 days). Dispatch ServiceAccountSecretRotationDueNotification to each SA's Application owners + team-wide DLs.
steps: SELECT id, application_id, expires_at FROM service_accounts WHERE is_enabled = true AND deleted_at IS NULL AND expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at BETWEEN now() AND now() + INTERVAL '$warningDays days'.
For each: fire ServiceAccountSecretRotationScheduled event (queue = notifications) which the notification-dispatch listener consumes.
Emit `academorix.service_account.rotation.warned` counter metric (label: application_id).
Update `svc:rotation_due_count:total` gauge with the total row count.

jobs.purpose: Daily (04:00 UTC) two-pass sweep. Pass 1: soft-disable SAs whose `expires_at + grace_days_after_expiry < now()` and are still enabled. Pass 2: hard-delete SAs whose `deleted_at + retention_hold_days < now()`.
steps: PASS 1 (expire): SELECT id FROM service_accounts WHERE is_enabled = true AND deleted_at IS NULL AND expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at + INTERVAL '$graceDays days' < now() LIMIT 200.

For each: UPDATE service_accounts SET is_enabled = false WHERE id = ?; fire ServiceAccountExpired event (with trigger='expired_grace' on subsequent Disabled event); emit `academorix.service_account.expired` metric.
PASS 2 (hard-delete): SELECT id FROM service_accounts WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at + INTERVAL '$retentionHoldDays days' < now() LIMIT 100.
For each: BEGIN TRANSACTION; DELETE FROM service_accounts WHERE id = ?; UPDATE audits SET user_id = '[REDACTED-SERVICE-ACCOUNT]' + anonymise user_type where user_id = ?; COMMIT.
Fire ServiceAccountDeleted(erasure_phase='hard_delete') event.
Emit `academorix.service_account.retention.purged` metric.
The signer_kid is NOT touched — it may live on to serve other SAs. The auth module owns its own kid retention policy.

jobs.purpose: Flush the coalesced `svc:last_used:{service_account_id}` cache buffer to Postgres. Runs every 30 seconds. Batches all pending updates into a single UPDATE ... FROM (VALUES ...) so the write pressure is bounded regardless of machine traffic volume.
steps: Read every `svc:last_used:*` key from the cache atomically (SCAN + MGET).
Parse into (service_account_id, last_used_at, last_used_ip) tuples.

UPDATE service_accounts SET last_used_at = <tuple.timestamp>, last_used_ip = <tuple.ip> FROM (VALUES ...) AS t(id, ts, ip) WHERE service_accounts.id = t.id AND (service_accounts.last_used_at IS NULL OR service_accounts.last_used_at < t.ts).
Delete the flushed cache keys atomically.
Emit `academorix.service_account.last_used.flush` metric with the batch size.

jobs.purpose: Weekly (Sundays 06:00 UTC) enumerate every SA whose `last_used_at` is older than `service-accounts.security.dormant_threshold_days` (default 30) AND is still enabled AND is older than the threshold (SAs created within the window are exempt — they're new). Dispatch ServiceAccountDormantNotification to each SA's Application owners + security-mailing-list.
steps: SELECT id, application_id, last_used_at, created_at FROM service_accounts WHERE is_enabled = true AND deleted_at IS NULL AND created_at < now() - INTERVAL '$thresholdDays days' AND (last_used_at IS NULL OR last_used_at < now() - INTERVAL '$thresholdDays days').
For each: dispatch ServiceAccountDormantNotification.
Emit `academorix.service_account.dormant.detected` counter metric (label: application_id).
Update `svc:dormant_count:total` gauge with the total row count.
